free_text: Activation cross section measurement at neutron
V.S.Shirley, ed., Table of isotopes, 7Th edition.
The TANDEM heavy ion acceler. at JAERI.
produce neutrons in the energy range  9.5 - 13.2 MeV.
The B-11 beams, accelerated to 45 - 68 MeV, were
incident upon a hydrogen gas target. Background runs
with empty gas cell were also performed. The
irradiations lasted for about 10 hours for both gas
The samples were 20 mm in diameter and 1 mm thick.
They were stacked in packages in the order of
Al/Zr/Nb/in for the irradiation at 13.2 MeV and
Al/Ti/Nb/Zn/in at 12.0 and 11.0 MeV. Two gold foils
for neutron flux monitoring were attached on front and
rear surfaces of each stack. The packages were placed
at a distance of about 100 mm from the center of the
The activity was measured with a Ge detector.
.A neutron flux monitor was used to detect fluctuations
flat response in the 10 - 14 MeV range.
Necessary corrections were applied for the activity
measurements. In addition, corrections were applied
for neutron flux fluctuations during the irradiations.
No corrections were applied for low-energy neutrons.
This is expected to be of importance only for the
The errors were composed of the following
Reaction rate of the monitor error
The uncertainty of mean neutron energy
Uncertainty of monitor reaction wasn't considered
DATA-ERR -> ERR-T, error structure is given
